component.name: Atrium
component.summary: Local-first GTD task manager with a native GNOME design

description.p: Atrium is a native GNOME task manager that fuses Things 3 clarity with OmniFocus depth via a mode switch over a shared local-first data store. Pick Simple Mode for what-am-I-doing-right-now; switch to Builder Mode when you want full GTD review, deferral, sequential projects, and forecast. Same data, two surfaces, no migration.

Storage is one SQLite file at $XDG_DATA_HOME/atrium/atrium.db, written by a single dedicated worker thread so the UI never blocks on I/O. The schema is the OmniFocus superset on day one — Simple Mode hides the Builder fields, it doesn't lack them. An optional Org-mode vault mirrors the database as round-trippable .org files so your data stays portable to Emacs, Logseq, or a pile of files in a git repo.
Simple Mode features (v0.1):

ul.li: Inbox · Today · Upcoming · Anytime · Someday · Logbook
Areas → Projects → Tasks hierarchy with multi-tag
Distinct When (scheduled-for) and Deadline dates
Quick Entry global modal (Ctrl+Alt+Space)
FTS5-backed search with filter expressions (tag:, is:, due:)
Multi-select bulk operations with undo (Ctrl+Z)
Find-as-you-type sidebar filter (Ctrl+L)
Keyboard-first — every common op bindable, mouse optional
Drag-reorder rows; drag tasks onto projects to file them
Local-first — no network, no telemetry, no accounts

description.p: Packaging fix. Several historical release entries in this metainfo file carried raw, unescaped angle brackets and an ampersand inside their description text, which made the file fail XML validation and would have blocked an AppStream / Flathub submission. Those characters are now properly escaped; the file validates cleanly. No code or behaviour changes.
Documentation and lint maintenance patch. No behaviour changes, no schema changes.

Doc accuracy: the spec header was pinned at v0.12.0 and is now current; the architecture section's crate count corrected from four to six (adding `atrium-org` and `atrium-inline`); stale internal source paths updated to reflect the v0.4.2 / v0.9.0 / v0.13.0 crate extractions.
Lint: behaviour-preserving clippy fixes across ten files (`map_or` / `is_some_and` / `is_none_or` rewrites, unnested or-patterns). Workspace stays at 888 tests with clippy `-D warnings` clean.
Maintenance pass after the rapid v0.14.0 → v0.20.0 sequence — no behaviour changes, no spec changes. Audited via clippy pedantic + unwrap census + long- function census + test-coverage gap pass.

Bag of small wins: migration `0013_task_clock_entry_timestamps.sql` closes an audit-trail gap (the only table that shipped without `created_at` / `modified_at`); stale Org parser docstring updated; Quick Entry shortcut sniff now validates ASCII alphanum at the GUI layer; reminder service consolidated `Utc::now()` calls; targeted clippy auto-fixes across 24 files (format string interpolation, redundant closures, `map_or`, let-else rewrites). `user_version` 12 → 13.

Refactors: worker dispatch loop reduced via 12 helper methods on `Worker` (`emit_*_created` / `_updated` / `_deleted` per kind) — each Command arm now uniformly 5–7 lines. `atrium-core/src/db/read.rs` (2288 lines) split into `read/{clock,counts,search,templates}.rs` sub-modules + a slimmer `read/mod.rs` (1780 lines). `atrium-cli/src/main.rs` (2604 lines) split with `clock.rs` + `template.rs` extracted (saved 449 lines in `main.rs`).
Test coverage: 34 new tests in `atrium-cli/src/args.rs` covering v0.17.0+ subcommands (clock, template, --deadline-warn). Workspace total now 888 tests (was 854).
Phase 19.5 foundations land: a real preferences dialog and the first system-notification surface. The two pair naturally — preferences exposes the toggle that gates the new reminder service.

Preferences (`AdwPreferencesDialog`, libadwaita 1.6+): three pages — General (default mode, theme override, high-legibility font, vault path with folder picker), Capture (Quick Entry shortcut), Notifications (master on/off). All keys write straight through to GSettings; the dialog is a thin presentation layer over the live settings backend. Wired to `app.preferences` (`Ctrl+Comma`) and the primary menu's "Preferences…" entry.

Reminder service: migration `0012_task_reminder_at.sql` adds `task.reminder_at` (`user_version` 11 → 12) plus a partial index on open future reminders. A single tokio task on the GLib MainContext polls the next pending reminder, sleeps until it fires, and emits a `gio::Notification` with the task title. Notifications open the inspector via the new `app.show-task::ID` parameterised action. The TaskChanges bridge wakes the service after every batch via `tokio::sync::Notify` so freshly-set reminders take effect without a timer wait. CLI: `--reminder "YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM"` flag on `add` (parsed as local time).
Phase 18.5 closes with both Tier-2 items: ID-based inter-task links and time-of-day on the schedule. All seven Phase 18.5 features now shipped across v0.14.0 → v0.19.0.

Org links: `[[id:UUID][label]]` in note bodies render as clickable spans (Inspector body editor); click navigates to the linked task. Builder Mode pane gets a "Link…" picker for inserting links via search-as-you-type. Zero schema impact (UUIDs round-tripped already).

Scheduled time: migration `0011_task_scheduled_time.sql` adds `task.scheduled_time` (`user_version` 10 → 11). Inspector pane gains a Time row visible only when scheduled is a Date. Forecast and Calendar Month View prefix scheduled rows with the time when present. Org parser/emitter round-trip the time through SCHEDULED active timestamps. Closes the Todoist mapper's `DroppedTimeOfDay` lossy entry. CLI: `--time HH:MM` flag.

Phase 18.5 Tier-1 Quick Entry templates — the fifth and final Tier-1 item. All five Phase 18.5 Tier-1 features (deadline warning windows, statistics cookies, body checkboxes, custom TODO sequences, CLOCK time tracking, and now Quick Entry templates) are now shipped.

Migration `0010_quick_entry_template.sql` adds the `quick_entry_template` table (`user_version` 9 → 10). The Quick Entry modal grows a picker bar above the entry — each configured template renders as a ToggleButton; clicking pre-fills the entry with the template's prefix and stashes its project + tags for commit. The Emacs-convention `:c ` shortcut trigger also activates templates mid-typing. CLI: `atrium-cli template list/add/edit/remove`.

Phase 18.5 Tier-1 CLOCK time tracking — the flagship Phase 18.5 feature. Records actual time spent on a task across multiple work sessions (distinct from `estimated_minutes` which is intent), with full round-trip to Org's `:LOGBOOK:` drawer so Emacs users see the same data.

Migration `0009_task_clock_entry.sql` adds the `task_clock_entry` side table (`user_version` 8 → 9). Worker enforces single-active-clock — clocking in on task B auto-closes any other running clock first (mirrors Emacs's global clock). Builder Mode Inspector pane gains a Time group with Start/Stop, total, and per-session log. CLI: `atrium-cli clock in/out/log` plus a bare `clock` for active-clock status. Org parser/emitter learn the `:LOGBOOK:` drawer; the writer suppresses in-progress entries to avoid file churn while a clock runs. The watcher syncs external Emacs clock-line edits back into the DB.

Phase 18.5 Tier-1 custom TODO sequences land. The most-cited Org feature (Bernt Hansen, Jethro Kuan, every GTD-with-Org tutorial) — per-vault declared keyword sets — round-trip end-to-end through Atrium's sync. Zero schema impact: sequences live in the sidecar (`[[todo_sequences]]` slot), and the existing `task.orig_keyword` column from v0.7.12 carries the labels through Atrium's TODO/DONE binary.

The vault writer projects each sequence as a `#+TODO: workflow | done` preamble per project file. The watcher maps in-set done keywords to Atrium's completed state with the source label preserved; out-of-set keywords surface a toast and stash via the existing Custom path (graceful degradation, never destroy data). Builder Mode Inspector pane gains a keyword picker visible only when a sequence is configured. CLI: `atrium-cli vault sequences list/set /clear --vault PATH`.

Two Phase 18.5 features bundled — statistics cookies on parent headlines (`[2/5]` / `[40%]`) and body inline checkboxes (`- [ ]` / `- [X]`). Karl Voit names both essential, and Org's `org-checkbox-hierarchical-statistics` folds the two into one unified count, so they ship together. Both modes; zero schema migrations.

The Org parser captures cookies from headlines and strips them from titles; the writer re-emits cookies projected from DB state on every flush, preserving the user's chosen shape (counter vs percent) across round-trips. New `atrium_core::checkbox` module surfaces a forgiving body-checkbox parser + toggle helper. The Inspector renders a "Subtasks" group above Notes listing each checkbox as a CheckButton; toggles rewrite the body in place. Cookie counter folds child TODOs + body checkboxes so a parent with both gets one unified [N/M]. Task list rows show the cookie inline between title and tags; tabular figures keep digits aligned when counts cross a digit boundary.

First Phase 18.5 item lands. The Org-mode `-Nd` warning suffix on the DEADLINE cookie (`DEADLINE: <2026-04-15 Wed -7d>` — "surface 7 days early") becomes a per-task override on the previously-global TODAY heads-up window, so a sensitive deadline can surface earlier than the default 7 days without changing how unmarked tasks behave.

Schema migration `0008_task_deadline_warn_days.sql` adds `task.deadline_warn_days` (`user_version` 7 → 8). Today and the sidebar badge use `COALESCE(deadline_warn_days, default)` so per-task overrides win without disturbing the global default. Builder Mode Inspector pane gains a SpinRow (visible only when a deadline is set) that writes through to the new column. atrium-cli accepts `--deadline-warn N` on `add` and `edit`. Org parser / emitter / watcher learn the warning shape — both `-` and `--` prefixes parse to the same value, the writer canonicalises onto `-`, and external Emacs edits flow back through the diff path. Test count: 11 new tests across parser, emitter, read layer, worker, and end-to-end watcher scenarios.

Hotfix on top of v0.13.4: the fresh-vault seed now registers its writes in the shared `RecentWrites` set that the writer + watcher consult to suppress self-induced echoes. v0.13.4 missed the handshake, so on every fresh-vault boot the watcher treated all of Atrium's seeded `.org` files as external edits and the writer flushed each one with a `<file>.atrium.bak. <UTC>` backup — surfacing as a flood of "vault conflict" warnings.

Fix exposes the shared filter set via a new `VaultLoopHandle::recent_writes()` accessor; the boot-path seed records each file's `(path, mtime)` immediately after writing it, closing the watcher's self-write window before the 200 ms debounce fires.

Fresh vaults seed on first boot. Phase 17's vault loop is change-driven — setting vault-path on an empty directory previously did nothing visible until the user edited a task. v0.13.4 detects a fresh vault by the absence of `.atrium/config.toml` and spawns a background task that dumps every project's .org file and writes the sidecar. Subsequent boots find the sidecar and skip the seed.

The seed runs on the existing tokio runtime so the GTK window appears within the spec §8 cold-start budget. Pre-existing .org files in a sidecar-less vault get backed up via the v0.10.1 conflict- detection path before being overwritten — hand-edits are never lost.

Stylistic Org-emit fix — successive headlines now have a blank line between them, matching Emacs's default `org-blank-before-new-entry` convention. DoomEmacs users opening Atrium's vault saw the cramping immediately; the fix makes Atrium-written files indistinguishable from Emacs-written ones. One-line change in emit_task. Round-trip discipline intact — the parser was already blank-tolerant.

Inline-rename gets the tab-completion popover. v0.13.0 Slice 3 wired the popover into the bottom-of-list capture entry and the Quick Entry modal but deliberately skipped the per-row inline- rename Entry. v0.13.2 closes that gap by attaching in the GtkSignalListItemFactory setup() callback — which runs once per row's lifetime, across recycles. The popover lives with the title_entry widget, not with whichever task is currently bound, so no teardown bookkeeping is needed.

build_factory gained a fourth argument — a closure that resolves the read pool lazily — so the factory can be built before attach_data_layer fires. Behaviour matches the other surfaces (Tab/Enter accept; ↓/↑ navigate; Esc dismisses popover only). Until the user enters edit mode the title_entry is invisible and the popover's listeners stay quiet — no overhead per paint.

Test count holds at 824 across the workspace; the popover wiring is GTK-side glue and underlying parser semantics are already covered by the existing atrium-inline + inline_complete tests. Schema unchanged at version 7. No new third-party crates.

Sidecar follow-up — saved Perspectives now round-trip through <vault>/.atrium/config.toml. The slot was reserved at v0.10.1 with a placeholder section; v0.13.1 ships the real format using TOML's array-of-tables shape (one block per perspective with name / filter / icon / renderer / renderer_config). Forward-compat default for renderer ("list" when omitted) and backward-compat for pre-v0.13.1 placeholder sidecars (still parse cleanly).

build_from_db reads list_perspectives in stored position order so the sidebar ordering survives the round-trip. The hand-rolled minimal-TOML parser learned one new shape — array-of-tables — and stays dependency-free.
Test count: 824 across the workspace (up from 817 at v0.13.0; 7 new sidecar tests including a worker-driven build_from_db acceptance test).

The atrium-inline arc — a polish + extraction cycle on top of v0.12.0's Phase 18 work. The inline-syntax parser (#tag, @today, etc.) was small in v0.1 and grew steadily through Phase 6c (Quick Entry) and Phase 18 (Todoist mapper); v0.13.0 unifies the vocabulary across every capture surface, expands it, lifts the parser out of atrium-core into its own workspace crate, and wires a tab-completion popover so the syntax becomes discoverable instead of memorised.

Slice 1 — inline rename routes through quick_entry. F2 / right-click → Rename / double-click into edit on a task row now runs the new title through the parser. A rename of "Wash dishes" to "Wash dishes #urgent @today" picks up the urgent tag plus a today schedule in one keystroke. Plain-text renames take a fast path identical to pre-v0.13 behaviour. Tags are added, never removed — the rename surface doesn't show existing tags, so a destructive merge would surprise users.

Slice 2 — !N priority and @<weekday> tokens. !1 / !2 / !3 set priority (Todoist convention, 1 = high). Strict 1-3 — Todoist's default 4 emits no token. @<weekday> (@mon / @monday, both case-insensitive, full names + tues / weds / thur / thurs aliases) sets scheduled_for to the next occurrence of that weekday on or after today. Today's weekday returns today (not next week). Single-valued priority semantics in the rename surface — !2 swaps any existing priority-* tag for priority-2 atomically without disturbing free-form #tag attachments.

Slice 3 — atrium-inline crate extraction plus tab-completion popover. The parser lives at atrium-inline now; atrium-core stays inline-syntax- agnostic. atrium-inline pulls atrium-core for ScheduledFor, never the reverse. Same shape as the v0.9.0 atrium-org extraction. New atrium_inline::completions module exposes context detection plus candidate filtering; the popover (atrium/src/ui/inline_complete.rs) is wired into the bottom-of-list new_task_entry and the Quick Entry modal. Tab and Enter accept the highlighted candidate; ↓ opens the popover when the cursor is on a recognised token; Escape dismisses without committing. Inline-rename's popover lifecycle defers to a v0.13.x patch.

Vocabulary curation: the popover surfaces full-name weekdays (monday … sunday) plus today / tomorrow / someday / deadline plus 1 / 2 / 3 priority levels. The 3-letter shortcuts stay parser-recognised but don't clutter the suggestion list. A vocabulary regression guard fails loudly if a new full-name keyword lands in the parser without being added to the candidate list.

spec.md §6 (Quick Entry) updated to document the v0.13 tokens and to surface the architectural commitment: the same parser, the same vocabulary, four surfaces (Quick Entry modal, bottom-of-list entry, inline-rename, CLI capture).

Test count: 817 across the workspace (up from 798 at v0.12.0). atrium-inline contributes 49 tests on its own; inline_complete adds 3 byte/char-conversion tests. Schema unchanged at version 7. No new third-party crates.

Phase 18 — Todoist CSV import. The cross-platform productivity app most likely-to-migrate Linux user is leaving behind now has a real export path into Atrium. New `atrium-cli import todoist PATH --into PROJECT_NAME [--dry-run]` reads a Todoist CSV export, walks its row stream, and materialises the project + sections + tasks + tags + recurrence rules through the single-writer worker.

Three hand-rolled stdlib parsers compose the pipeline. The CSV parser tolerates UTF-8 BOMs, quoted fields with embedded commas, escaped double-quotes, and blank separator rows; classifies each row by its TYPE column into Meta / Section / Task / Blank. The natural-language recurrence parser turns Todoist's loose phrasing ("Every Sunday at 10am", "every 3 day at 9am" with the singular typo, "Every 1stday", "3 days ago at 15:00") into RFC 5545 RRULE strings plus a scheduled anchor; six pattern-matchers cover every phrasing in the Home.csv fixture plus sensible extensions. The mapper layer turns the parsed row stream into worker calls — section rows become heading rows, task rows become tasks with parent_id chains driven by the INDENT column, @label tokens strip from the title and become Atrium tags, PRIORITY 1-3 emits a priority-N tag (4 is Todoist's default and emits no tag).

Phase 18 prerequisites in atrium-core + atrium-org: WorkerHandle::ensure_heading is the new idempotent heading-create-by-(project, title-NOCASE) API; the Org writer learned to emit heading rows as depth-1 keyword-less headlines and interleave them with top-level tasks by `position`, so a vault file opens in DoomEmacs as a section-bearing project rather than a flat list. New name-based v5 UUID namespace gives tasks deterministic IDs derived from `(project_name, label-stripped title)` so re-runs onto the same project produce stable `:ID:` round-trips.

The closing acceptance gate is the home.csv "butter test": the sanitised version of Brandon's daughter Rin's chore-tracker (10 sections, 46 tasks, deeply- nested shopping list, weekly + every-3-days recurrence, two distinct labels) round-trips Todoist → DB → vault → re-parse without losing data or scrambling structure. Embedded commas in titles survive, recurring tasks carry both an Org cookie on SCHEDULED and the canonical :RRULE: in the drawer, @chore / @home labels emit as Org headline tags, and no @-prefixed leftovers remain in any title.

New deps: uuid v5 feature flag (additive — pulls in sha1_smol via the existing uuid crate) for deterministic name-based UUIDs. atrium-cli grew direct uuid + thiserror entries (was transitive). No new third-party crates.
Test count: 798 across the workspace (up from 765 at v0.11.0). Schema unchanged at version 7. The regression gate stays under 2 seconds.

Phase 12.5 — Calendar Month View. Builder Mode gains the third lens over the same task data Forecast (30- day strip) and Agenda (chronological bands) already cover: a paper-calendar grid for users who think in calendar pages. Re-engaged from its earlier "subsumed by Agenda" framing — the lens is genuinely different.

New atrium/src/ui/calendar.rs ships pure date-math helpers (first_of_month, grid_anchor, week_rows, previous_month / next_month, build_month_grid) and the GTK widget tree (build_page producing a 7×N grid with day cells: day number + count badge + up to 3 inline task titles + "+N more" overflow popover). Today's cell highlighted; out-of-month leading / trailing days muted; ISO Mon-start week alignment matching Atrium's Agenda buckets.

Drag-to-reschedule between days mirrors Forecast's pattern: each task title is a DragSource carrying the task id; each cell is a DropTarget that updates scheduled_for via the worker. Out-of-month cells accept drops too so users can drag into the next or previous month from leading / trailing rows.

Click-day-to-filter: single-click opens a peek popover with the day's full task list (each task is a flat button that opens the inspector); double-click drills into a date-scoped search via "scheduled:YYYY- MM-DD" so users get the full editing surface (drag, multi-select, complete) on the standard list view.

Narrow-window collapse: below the 600 px width threshold the month grid swaps for a vertical week strip — 7 day cards stacked vertically, focused on the week containing today, with full task lists inline. The window watches its own default-width and rebuilds the calendar when the threshold flips.

Builder-only: sidebar entry "Calendar" sits between Forecast and Review in Builder Mode; mode-flip filters it out in Simple. Ctrl+Shift+M opens the page from anywhere; Page Up / Page Down step months when the calendar has focus.

13 calendar lib tests cover the date-math edge cases: month boundaries, leap February, DST transitions (March / November 2026), short / long months, year wrap on prev/next, today-cell marking, out-of-month flagging, completed-task and deadline- only-task exclusion (calendar shows scheduled tasks only — the paper-calendar idiom).
Test count: 650 across the workspace (up from 637 at v0.10.3). Schema unchanged at version 7. No new third-party dependencies.

Phase 17 closer: RRULE canonicalisation contract end- to-end. Repeating tasks now emit both a best-fit Org cookie and the canonical :RRULE: in the property drawer; the watcher detects when a user edits the cookie alone in Emacs and rewrites the file so the cookie matches the canonical rule (DB stays canonical). Plus the agenda-parity acceptance test gates Phase 17's closure.

New atrium-org/src/rrule_cookie.rs ships three pure helpers: rrule_to_org_cookie / rrule_to_org_repeater project the canonical RRULE down to the cookie (multi-weekday and BYMONTHDAY patterns degrade to "nearest interval" per spec §7.3.3 rule 3); the inverse org_repeater_to_rrule reconstructs the rule the cookie implies; cookie_matches_rrule is the equality check used by divergence detection. Hand-rolled FREQ + INTERVAL parser; no toml-style dependency.

Writer wiring: scheduled_repeater_from_task now reads task.repeat_rule + repeat_mode and produces the cookie via rrule_to_org_repeater. The v0.7.10 None- returning placeholder finally flips on. SCHEDULED lines now emit `<2026-05-11 Mon ++1w>` for repeating tasks; the canonical :RRULE: still lives in the property drawer as the source of truth.

Watcher fixes two related v0.10.0 → v0.10.2 gaps. The :RRULE: property is now read on create (to_new_task) and synced on update (diff_from + TaskUpdate.repeat_ rule_value); a user editing :RRULE: in Emacs now propagates to DB. Divergence detection: when the cookie disagrees with :RRULE:, surface VaultEvent::RruleDiverged and synchronously rewrite the file from DB.

Phase 17 closing acceptance test: synthesised vault with tasks across every bucket (Overdue / Today / Tomorrow / This Week / Next Week + every "shouldn't appear" edge case) plus a spec-derived reference classifier; both classifiers must agree on every task. Visual style differs from stock org-agenda; semantic groupings agree.

Phase 17 (vault → DB two-way sync) is closed. All roadmap §17 checkboxes tick. v0.11 opens Phase 18 (Todoist CSV); Phase 12.5 (Calendar Month View) is re-engaged from its earlier "subsumed by Agenda" framing.
Test count: 637 across the workspace (up from 616 at v0.10.2). Schema unchanged at version 7. No new third-party dependencies.

Phase 17 reliability slice: malformed vault files no longer spam toasts; custom Org keywords (WAITING, IN-PROGRESS, BLOCKED) round-trip cleanly through the watcher path; vault file deletion preserves tasks with a toast warning instead of silently leaving stale rows. Plus three test scenarios from spec §17.

Malformed-file pause/resume: when the watcher hits a parse error on a vault file, sync pauses for that file until it parses cleanly again. The user sees one ParseFailed toast on the pause transition, then quiet until a ParseRecovered toast confirms sync resumed. Repeated bad saves no longer re-toast on every inotify event.

Custom-keyword fix: the v0.10.0 watcher dropped Org keywords other than CANCELLED on its create path entirely (a fresh WAITING headline appearing in the vault landed in DB as plain TODO), and TaskUpdate had no orig_keyword field so external keyword changes on existing rows didn't sync at all. Both fixed. TaskUpdate.orig_keyword + builder method added; the watcher's create + update paths route through a shared helper.

Vault file removal: when a user `rm`s a vault file or moves it out of the vault, Atrium now retains the project's tasks per spec §3.5 (DB canonical, vault projected) and surfaces a toast. A stray rm no longer silently leaves stale rows; the next project flush recreates the file from DB.

Test scenarios: synthesized concurrent edit (Atrium + external save racing) confirms the writer-side conflict detection catches both timings; 1K-task project parses in well under the 500 ms budget; new VaultEvent variants ParseRecovered + FileRemoved bridge through to GUI toasts with distinct copy.
Test count: 616 across the workspace (up from 611 at v0.10.1). Schema unchanged at version 7. No new third-party dependencies.

Phase 17 next slice: GUI now participates in the two-way vault loop. The GTK binary boots with both the writer half (DB → vault, since v0.7.16) and the watcher half (vault → DB, new at v0.10.0) wired up — a save in Doom Emacs lands in Atrium's task list within ~250 ms.

Conflict detection (spec §7.3.3 rule 5): when the writer is about to overwrite a vault file whose mtime it doesn't recognise as its own, the current contents are first snapshotted to <file>.atrium.bak.<UTC-timestamp>. The user's external edit survives the overwrite; a toast surfaces in the GUI naming both files.

Sidecar config (Phase 16 carryover): tag colours round-trip through <vault>/.atrium/config.toml. Hand-rolled minimal TOML; refreshed by the writer after every flush burst that changes tag state, idle otherwise. Mode and saved-perspective slots are reserved for follow-up.
VaultEvent channel: writer + watcher operational events (ConflictBackup, ParseFailed) flow back to the GUI for toast surfacing through a new spawn_vault_loop builder.

Worker now enforces two domain invariants the schema couldn't: subtasks must live in the same project as their parent (rejected on create_task and on update_task project moves) and saved perspectives must have a non-empty filter expression. New DomainError type wired through DbError; UiError + AtriumError replace the four-year-old #![allow(dead_code)] scaffolding in the GTK binary.

Bug fix: the v0.10.0 vault watcher's flatten_one silently dropped TODOs nested under non-keyword headings (e.g. * Backlog / ** TODO Real task). The importer always handled this correctly; the watcher now matches.
Test count: 611 across the workspace (up from 590). No new third-party dependencies.

Phase 17 first slice: vault → DB sync. Edits made in Emacs / Doom / vim-orgmode against the configured Org vault now flow back into the SQLite store within ~250 ms. Closes the loop opened by Phase 16's DB → vault writer at v0.8.0.

The watcher: new atrium-org/src/vault_watcher.rs hosts a tokio task using the notify crate (sign-off granted) to subscribe to .org file events. Debounced 200 ms, pairs with the existing VaultWriter, submits diff results through the existing WorkerHandle.

The self-write filter: shared RecentWrites set keyed on (path, mtime_just_written) — exact tuple equality rather than path-only TTL. The first design used path+TTL and the integration tests immediately surfaced its failure mode (external edits within the TTL window got swallowed). Linux ext4's nanosecond mtime gives perfect discrimination between Atrium- from-Atrium echoes and real external edits.

The diff: matches parsed tasks to DB tasks by :ID:. CREATE / UPDATE / DELETE submitted through WorkerHandle. Headlines added externally without :ID: get a fresh UUIDv4; the writer's auto rewrite stamps the property onto disk and the self-write filter swallows the resulting echo. New TaskUpdate.completed_at setter so CLOSED: cookies round-trip into the DB verbatim instead of stamping now() through toggle.

Three integration tests pin the working slice end-to- end: external add, external edit (TODO → DONE), and external delete. Conflict detection (mtime race), GUI wiring, malformed-file pause/resume, RRULE divergence detection, and the agenda-parity acceptance test land across the v0.10.x patch arc.

590 tests across the workspace, all green. Schema unchanged at version 7. New direct dependency in atrium-org: notify v8 (the canonical Rust file- watching crate; cross-platform, but Atrium ships Linux-only — the inotify backend is what we exercise).

atrium-org crate extraction. The Phase 16 Org projection — parser, emitter, importer, vault writer task — moves out of atrium-core::sync into its own workspace crate, atrium-org. atrium-core stays Org- agnostic; the worker hooks into the projection through a new VaultDirtyNotifier trait. Workspace is now five crates (atrium-core, atrium-search, atrium-org, atrium-cli, atrium).

What moved: atrium-core/src/sync/org/* and atrium-core/src/sync/vault_writer.rs into atrium-org. The org-related integration tests moved with them. What stayed: atrium-core/src/sync/atomic.rs (write- temp + fsync + rename helper) and atrium-core/src/sync/json.rs (lossless DB snapshot).

The trait abstraction: atrium-core's worker holds an Option<Arc<dyn VaultDirtyNotifier>> instead of a concrete mpsc::Sender. atrium-org's OrgVaultNotifier wraps the sender and provides the impl. Convenience helper atrium_org::spawn_org_vault returns a ready-to-use VaultConfig so GUI and CLI boot paths stay one-call.
Pre-Phase-17 housekeeping. No behaviour change, no schema change, no new dependencies. 582 tests, all green.

Phase 16 stamp + maintenance pass. End of the eleven-patch v0.7.6 → v0.7.18 Phase 16 build-out. Atrium's GTK binary, atrium-cli, and the hand-rolled atrium-core::sync::org parser/emitter together let a user keep a vault at the configured path, edit tasks in Atrium, and see the .org files reflect the change inside ~150 ms — readable in stock org-agenda, Doom, vim-orgmode, or any other Org-aware tool.

v0.8.0 ships the maintenance pass per release discipline: worker.rs test split into worker_tests.rs (2622 → 1469 lines source + 1161 lines tests), dead- code prune in the Org writer's build_org_tree, and a comment audit that stripped per-patch v0.7.X markers from atrium-core/src/sync and atrium-core/src/db (74 → 26 surviving markers). Four-doc sweep for spec, roadmap, README, and CLAUDE.md.
582 tests across the four crates, all green. Schema unchanged at version 7. No new dependencies. Phase 17 (vault → DB inotify-driven sync) is what's next.

GUI vault integration. Thirteenth patch on the Phase 16 arc. The GTK binary now reads the vault-path GSettings key on boot and routes through spawn_worker_with_vault when the key is non-empty, so every DB write auto-flushes to the projected .org files in the configured vault.

boot_data_layer reads the key, builds a VaultConfig if set (auto-creating the directory if missing), and passes it into atrium-core's worker spawn. Empty key falls through to DB-only mode. Misconfigured paths log a tracing::warn and fall back gracefully so the app never refuses to start over a vault config.

End-to-end smoke verified: tasks created through the GUI auto-flush to .org files within ~150 ms. A graphical Settings → Org Vault → Choose folder UI defers to Phase 19.5 alongside the rest of the AdwPreferencesWindow work; for now users set the key via `gsettings set io.github.virinvictus.atrium vault-path /path`.

Round-trip test fixture + two importer fixes. Twelfth patch on the Phase 16 arc. Five complicated .org files (kitchen_sink, custom_keywords, deep_nesting, project_metadata, unicode) round-trip through the full importer + writer + parser pipeline, asserting AST equality between source and regenerated trees.

The fixture surfaced two real importer gaps that v0.7.17 fixes: NewTask gains completed_at: Option<DateTime> so the importer threads source CLOSED cookies through verbatim instead of stamping now(); CANCELLED keywords now stash in orig_keyword so they survive the round-trip (Atrium's domain has TODO / DONE only; the orig_keyword anchor preserves the original label).
582 tests green (up 5 from v0.7.16). Pure additive change; no schema, no new dependencies. v0.7.18 wires the GUI vault integration; v0.8.0 stamps the arc.

Auto-debounced worker write hook (DB → vault). The last Phase 16 roadmap bullet before the v0.8.0 stamp. Every Task / Project write through the SQLite worker now triggers a background rewrite of the affected project's .org file in the configured vault.

New atrium-core::sync::vault_writer module hosts a VaultWriter task — receives ProjectDirty(project_id) requests, debounces ~100 ms to coalesce bursts, flushes through write_project_to_vault on a 50 ms tick. Latency upper bound from DB write to .org file landing: ~150 ms.

Worker gains spawn_with_vault(conn, vault: Option) + VaultConfig { root, read_pool }. spawn(conn) delegates with vault: None so atrium-cli and tests stay unchanged. Every dispatch site that mutates a project (CreateTask / UpdateTask / ToggleComplete / DeleteTask / CreateProject / UpdateProject / ArchiveProject / MarkReviewed / MarkTaskReviewed / SetTaskTags) calls notify_project_dirty.
577 tests green (up 3 from v0.7.15). Phase 16 roadmap bullets are now all complete; v0.8.0 wraps the arc with the Phase 16 stamp + round-trip test fixture + maintenance pass + GUI vault integration.

Post-write Org integrity check. Tenth patch on the Phase 16 arc. Every vault write now goes through a post-write parse-back assertion: the file Atrium just wrote must re-read cleanly through Atrium's own reader. If it doesn't, the emit returns an io::Error::Other and a tracing::warn lands in the log so the failure is visible.

Rollback to .atrium.bak.<timestamp> is the second half of the spec rule and lands with the auto-debounced worker write hook in v0.7.16+. v0.7.15 ships the lightweight detection layer; the v0.7.16 hook can make recovery decisions on top of the Err return.
574 tests green (up 1 from v0.7.14). Pure additive change; no schema, no new dependencies.

Multi-file vault walk + ensure_area. Ninth patch on the Phase 16 arc. atrium-cli `import org PATH` now accepts a directory: files at `vault/project.org` import as unfiled projects; files at `vault/area/project.org` create or reuse the named Area via the new WorkerHandle::ensure_area idempotent-create helper (mirror of ensure_tag, case-insensitive dedup). Hidden directories (.atrium, .git) and non-.org files are skipped; sub-area nesting beyond one level surfaces a warning per spec §7.3.1.
End-to-end verified: a 3-file vault imports as 3 projects + 2 auto-created areas + 2 auto-created tags. 573 tests green (up 2 from v0.7.13). Pure additive change; no schema, no new dependencies.

File-level Org metadata round-trip. Eighth patch on the Phase 16 arc. v0.7.12 closed the per-task half of the round-trip discipline; v0.7.13 closes the per-project half. An .org file's #+TITLE: directive plus a top-level :PROPERTIES: block carrying :ID: / :SEQUENTIAL: / :REVIEW_INTERVAL: / :LAST_REVIEWED: / :ARCHIVED: now round-trips cleanly through import → DB → export.

Parser gains additive parse_org_text_with_meta / parse_org_file_with_meta returning an OrgFile struct (directives + file_properties + headlines). Legacy parse_org_text/_file keep their Vec<OrgTask> shape for backwards compat. Emitter gains the matching with_meta variants. NewProject gains last_reviewed_at and archived_at fields to thread the imported values into the worker.
571 tests green (up 7 from v0.7.12). Pure additive change; no schema, no new dependencies.

Custom-keyword Org round-trip via migration 0007. Seventh patch on the Phase 16 arc. Spec §7.3.3 rule 1 calls for non-canonical TODO keywords (WAITING, BLOCKED, IN-PROGRESS, etc.) to round-trip cleanly. v0.7.12 implements this at the data-model level via a new task.orig_keyword TEXT NULL column. The Org importer stashes the original; the writer consults the column when emitting so the original keyword survives a vault round-trip. Atrium's UI never surfaces the column — completion semantics still flow through completed_at alone.
End-to-end test verifies WAITING / IN-PROGRESS / TODO survive an import → DB → export round-trip. user_version 6 → 7. 564 tests green (up 2). No new dependencies.

JSON snapshot export. Sixth patch on the Phase 16 arc. The Org vault projection is interoperable with Emacs but lossy on constructs Atrium doesn't fully model; v0.7.11 adds a complementary lossless format per the roadmap requirement.

atrium-core::sync::json::Snapshot serializes every relevant table (areas, projects, headings, tasks, tags, task_tags, perspectives) into one JSON document with version + exported_at + atrium_version metadata. Three new read primitives (list_all_projects including archived, list_headings, list_task_tags) support a complete backup. atrium-cli gains `export json PATH [--dry-run]` mirroring the export org shape.
562 tests green (up 4 from v0.7.10). Pure additive change; no schema, no new dependencies (serde_json already in the workspace set since Phase 1).

Vault writer + atrium-cli export org. Fifth patch on the Phase 16 arc. write_project_to_vault reads a project + every task + tag names through a read-only Connection, builds an OrgTask tree mirroring the spec §7.3.2 field mapping in reverse, and emits via the v0.7.8 atomic-write helper. write_all_projects_ to_vault walks list_projects.

atrium-cli gains `export org PATH [--dry-run]`. Dry- run reports what would be written without touching disk; JSON output for scripts. Round-trip verified end-to-end via a smoke test that imports a sample.org, exports the DB to a fresh directory, and the resulting file re-parses to the same task tree.
Project sub-headings (heading table writes), custom keyword preservation, file-level project metadata, and the auto-debounced worker write hook ship in v0.7.11+.
558 tests green (up 7 from v0.7.9). Pure additive change; no schema, no new dependencies.

Org importer (atrium-cli import org). Fourth patch on the Phase 16 arc. import_org_file reads a single .org file and inserts every TODO-keyworded headline as a task, mapping fields per spec §7.3.2: title, body verbatim, headline :tags: → Atrium tags via ensure_tag, SCHEDULED / DEADLINE → date fields, :ID: → task UUID (preserved via the new additive NewTask.uuid field), :RRULE: → repeat_rule, :EFFORT: → estimated_minutes, :DEFER_UNTIL: → defer_until, subtasks → parent_id.
atrium-cli gains `import org PATH [--dry-run]`. Dry- run walks the parse tree and tallies counts without touching the DB. JSON output for scripts.

Limitations deferred to v0.7.10+: project sub-headings are skipped (headings_skipped count); DONE tasks have completed_at = now() rather than the CLOSED cookie timestamp; multi-file vault walk; re-import upsert.
551 tests green (up 9 from v0.7.8). Pure additive change; no schema, no new dependencies.

Org-mode emitter (round-trip safe). Third patch on the Phase 16 arc. emit_org_text takes a Vec<OrgTask> and writes the canonical Org form: headline + cookies below + :PROPERTIES: drawer + body verbatim + children recursively at depth+1. emit_org_file goes through the v0.7.6 atomic-write helper so a crash mid-write leaves the previous file intact.

13 round-trip tests parse a representative input, emit, re-parse, and assert tree-equality. Coverage: every keyword, every repeater mode, headline tags, properties drawer, body preservation, nested subtasks, project sub-headings, custom keywords, unknown-lines preservation, and a kitchen-sink test combining everything.

Test count: 119 + 210 + 1 + 106 + 106 = 542 (up 18 from v0.7.7). Pure additive change; no schema, no new dependencies. Importer (Org → DB), writer (DB → Org), worker hook, and JSON export remain in the Phase 16 arc.

Hand-rolled Org-mode parser. Second patch on the Phase 16 arc. atrium-core::sync::org::parse_org_text reads Org text into a Vec<OrgTask> tree covering the spec §7.3 surface: headlines with depth-based subtask nesting, TODO / DONE / CANCELLED keywords (custom keywords like WAITING preserved verbatim), SCHEDULED / DEADLINE / CLOSED cookies, +/++/.+ repeater suffixes, headline tags, :PROPERTIES: drawer with case-preserving keys, and headline body captured verbatim so source blocks, tables, latex, and custom drawers round-trip cleanly.

The "preserve unknown constructs verbatim" rule from spec §7.3.3 is satisfied at two layers — body content stays verbatim; properties drawer entries that don't pattern-match land in unknown_lines for explicit round-trip.
Test count: 119 + 192 + 1 + 106 + 106 = 524 (up 18 from v0.7.6 — the new parser tests). Pure additive change; no schema, no new dependencies. Emitter, importer, writer follow in v0.7.8+.

Phase 16 foundation — first patch on the Org-mode vault projection arc that ships across v0.7.6 → v0.8.0. Lands the sync module skeleton (atrium-core/src/sync/), an atomic write-temp + fsync + rename helper for vault writes, and the vault-path GSettings key (empty default = "no vault configured"; Atrium runs DB-only without one).

Reversed the original plan to take an Org parsing crate. The v0.7.6 dep survey found orgize's last stable was four years old (active line has been in alpha since 2023) and starsector — the obvious alternative — was effectively abandoned and pulled orgize-alpha transitively anyway. Atrium hand-rolls the Org subset instead, fitting the stdlib-only ethos. The "preserve unknown constructs verbatim" rule (spec §7.3.3) becomes easier in a passthrough parser, not harder.
Five new atomic-write tests, all green. No schema changes; no new dependencies. Parser + importer + writer follow in v0.7.7+.

Visual refinement pass — five items finishing the v0.7.x polish arc. Tag pills retired the chip background in favour of bare colored Pango spans (the per-tag colour still renders, just as inline typography rather than a Bootstrap-style badge). The inspector's "No task selected" empty state swapped a giant AdwStatusPage for a small centred caption near the top of the pane. The sidebar filter entry gained the same ghost treatment the quick-add entry got at v0.7.0 (dim at rest, full on hover / focus). Task-list row separators faded further (alpha 0.30 → 0.12) so they hint without outshouting the v0.7.0 row spacing. Sidebar :selected rows gained rounded corners + horizontal margin so the fill blooms rather than clipping at the listbox edge.
Pure CSS + small UI tweaks. No schema changes; no new dependencies. 503 tests still green.

Task-level Mark Reviewed. The Review page's "This week" weekly walk shipped at v0.7.2 with no way to acknowledge an item; v0.7.4 closes the gap with a true Mark Reviewed action mirroring the Phase 13 project-level pattern.

Migration 0006_task_last_reviewed_at.sql adds an additive task.last_reviewed_at TEXT NULL column (mirror of project.last_reviewed_at). The MarkTaskReviewed worker command stamps it; rows in the weekly walk gain a trailing flat Mark Reviewed button. Clicking the button hides the row from the walk for 7 days. After 7 days the row resurfaces if it still matches the weekly-walk filter.
503 tests green (up from 501 — two new worker tests for MarkTaskReviewed round-trip + not-found). Pure additive change.

Inspector check-off + perspective editor. Two functional gaps after v0.7.2: the inspector had no way to mark a task complete (bouncing back to the row was required), and there was no GUI path to create or edit a saved perspective (atrium-cli was the only way).

Inspector check-off: a circular CheckButton at the leading edge of the title row mirrors the row- checkbox. State reflects completion; clicks toggle via worker.toggle_complete. Reachable while the inspector is open without leaving the pane.

Perspective editor: a new dialog covers name + filter + renderer (List / Board radios) + columns in one place. A "+" affordance next to the Perspectives sidebar header opens it in create mode. The right-click context menu on a perspective row now collapses Rename + Configure-renderer into one "Edit…" item that opens the same dialog pre-filled for editing. Delete remains on the shared confirmation flow.
Pure code patch. No schema changes, no new dependencies. 501 tests still green.

Confusion-killer patch. Two problems from Brandon's after-v0.7.1 review: the canonical Review page and the seeded "Weekly Review" perspective lived side-by-side in the sidebar under almost the same name and showed completely different content; and the upper-left corner still had the centered "Lists" header from libadwaita's default sidebar auto-title.

v0.7.2 fixes both. The canonical Review page now renders two sections — "Projects to review" (the Phase 13 review queue) and "This week" (the open-tasks-this-week filter formerly seeded as the Weekly Review perspective). Section 2 reuses the Agenda page's task-row treatment for visual consistency; clicking a row opens the Inspector. The seeded perspective is retired (existing user DBs keep their row; fresh DBs land clean). The sidebar's "Lists" auto-title is suppressed via an empty title-widget, mirroring the title-suppression v0.7.0 applied to the content side.
Pure code patch. No schema changes, no new dependencies. 501 tests green (down 4 from v0.7.1's 505 because the seeded-perspective tests are gone).

Surface continuity patch — kills the visible "colour breaking" Brandon flagged after v0.7.0. The upper-left corner showed three stacked horizontal bands of slightly different tone where the headerbar, filter entry, and listbox met. Three things were stacking: the v0.6.10 headerbar accent gradient (still painting on every headerbar), the libadwaita- default elevated headerbar bg, and the v0.7.0 directional surface gradients (which painted only on the inner widgets, not on the headerbar above).

v0.7.1 retires the standalone headerbar gradient, flattens the main-window headerbars to transparent so the surface flows behind them, and replaces the directional 160deg / -20deg gradients with a flat low-alpha accent tint applied to the whole sidebar / inspector pane. Inner widgets become transparent so the tint shows through continuously. The sidebar now reads as one warmer surface from top to bottom, the inspector mirrors it on the opposite side, and the neutral content area is the calm centre. No bands.

Trade-off: the directional gradient is gone. The flat tint is more Things-3-calm than OF4-atmospheric. The visible-banding cost wasn't worth the directional warmth. Pure CSS + window.ui patch; no code changes.

Visual fusion + whitespace pass. Addresses a critical-eye review of v0.6.21: the app didn't feel "living" yet — accents had hard boundaries, the three panes were visually identical rectangles separated by 1 px verticals, selection states read as outlines instead of glows, and Linux-app disregard for whitespace had crept into the row rhythm and the inspector. Two tiers shipped:

Tier 1 (living surface): three-pane atmosphere (sidebar + inspector now both carry matching low- alpha accent gradients on opposing edges, framing a neutral content area); selection state on task rows reworked from a 1 px bordered rectangle to a soft accent fill (selection becomes a glow, not a frame); area accent moved from a 3 px hard left stripe to a row-wide gradient bleed; sidebar section headers softened (no more all-caps + divider — medium weight, mixed case, breathing room); the "Add task…" quick-add row is now a ghost that materialises on hover or focus.

Tier 2 (whitespace): task-row vertical margins bumped 6 → 9 px so rows breathe; inspector pane field clustering tightened (Project + Tags now share one Classify cluster instead of Tags being a one-row orphan card); page title moved from the centered AdwHeaderBar slot to a magazine-spread strip below the header bar — large left-aligned heading with an optional subtitle (today's date for Today, "Next 7 days" for Upcoming, "Next 30 days" for Forecast).
No schema changes. No new dependencies. All 505 tests still green.

Documentation housekeeping pass. README version badge refreshed to 0.6.20. CLAUDE.md status section consolidated and codebase map updated to v0.6.20 (added the missing files from the search-engine extraction, the kanban renderer, the Agenda page, and migrations 0004 and 0005). docs/schema.md dropped the obsolete "no mid-v0.1 schema changes" framing, picked up a migration-history table, and the ER diagram now covers area.color, task.repeat_mode, and the perspective entity in full. docs/perf-baseline.md refreshed against current binaries — cold start and fixture generation remain inside the spec budget by a wide margin. docs/regression.md documents the atrium-cli end-to-end + kanban + perspective-write smokes. docs/keymap.md retired the "view lands Phase 5" markers, rewrote the search-filter section against the current expression-language grammar, and reframed the Builder Mode chord table as aspirational slots. Test counts updated from "248 as of v0.4.0" to "505 as of v0.6.20." Pure docs change; no code touched. Ship-gate green.

Phase 19.5 calendar-overlay item course-corrected from "read-only iCal feed" to "Evolution Data Server overlay." Atrium is a GNOME-native client on Fedora; the right integration model isn't a .ics file feed but reading the system's calendar service. GNOME 50's default calendar (`gnome-calendar`) consumes Evolution Data Server, which the user has already configured with their Google / Nextcloud / local / Exchange accounts via GNOME Online Accounts. Atrium will read EDS via D-Bus and overlay events onto Forecast / Today as read-only context. Pure docs change.

Roadmap revision. The Things 3 import phase is retired (`.things` JSON requires macOS export the typical Linux user can't produce; the GNOME-using- Things-3-user audience is too narrow). Org-mode promoted to Phase 16 + 17 as Atrium's must-ship two-way mirror — open the same vault in Emacs and org-agenda there should look like Atrium's Agenda canonical page here. Todoist promoted to its own Phase 18 (was buried in 19's long-tail). New Phase 19.5 covers nine productivity essentials surfaced by a feature-survey pass against Errands, Planify, Endeavour, Things 3, OmniFocus, Taskwarrior, and Todoist: system notifications, subtasks UI, Evolution Data Server calendar overlay, AdwPreferencesWindow, task dependencies, drag-drop external capture, templates, first-run onboarding, backup UI. Pure docs change; no code touched.

Efficiency pass — SQL fast-path everywhere search runs. v0.5.3 shipped the SQL translation evaluator and v0.6.6 wired it into the kanban refresh; v0.6.18 closes the deferred-case loop by wiring it into the regular list-renderer perspective path AND the search-bar SearchResults path. Translatable filters (most: is:open, tag:work, due:today, …) now load only matching rows from SQLite on every refresh instead of pulling every task and filtering in Rust. Plus eliminated a duplicate tag-map DB query on those refreshes — fetch tag_info once and derive the name-only view locally. Untranslatable expressions still take the in-memory fallback; semantics unchanged.

Forecast view click-to-open. Brandon flagged that clicking a task in Forecast did nothing. The row had a DragSource (so drag-to-reschedule worked) but no GestureClick. v0.6.17 adds the same click-to-Inspector callback the board and agenda rows already use; GTK4's drag-threshold lets the click and drag controllers coexist on the same row. List / kanban / agenda / forecast all open Inspector on single-click now.

Sidebar order — Logbook bookends the top tier. Brandon flagged that Logbook between Someday and Agenda read as out of place; completed work was interrupting the flow of active / future-facing lists. v0.6.16 moves Logbook to the trailing slot so the top tier reads "now → future → past" top to bottom: Inbox, Today, Upcoming, Anytime, Someday, Agenda, Forecast (Builder), Review (Builder), Logbook. Logbook keeps its accent tint and live count badge.

Two debug-surface bugfixes from a v0.6.14 eyes-on test. The Memory Watch dialog had no visible body / background — labels appeared to float against the desktop because adw::Window with an AdwToolbarView content slot wasn't painting its @window_bg_color. CSS fix. And the Debug → Generate Fixtures menu action wrote rows but never told the GUI to re-read; the sidebar stayed empty until the user re-launched the binary. The action now refreshes the window after the spawn_blocking write completes.

Patch D, reframed — visible row separators and a derived recurrence icon. The original "day-band grouping in the main task list" plan didn't fit any view well (Today is single-day; Logbook and Agenda already group); reframed as two smaller polish wins. Row separators get a 1px @borders-tinted bottom border so the rows read as distinct items rather than a wall of text. Tasks with a repeat_rule show a small refresh icon at the row's right edge — derived from data, not from title text. Closes the four-patch screenshot-cleanup arc that started at v0.6.11.

Patch C — Inspector Notes placeholder. The Notes field used to be a blank dark rectangle; first-run users had no way to know it was editable. v0.6.13 adds a placeholder hint ("What / why / next step — autosaves on focus-out") that disappears the moment the user types. Standard GTK4 overlay-Label idiom; autosave-on-focus-out behaviour is unchanged.

Patch B — state-aware row treatment. The biggest visual win in the screenshot-cleanup arc. Each task row classifies into Overdue / Today / Upcoming / Neutral based on its dates + completion state, and the leading checkbox + the right-hand schedule / deadline pills tint accordingly: strong red on overdue, warm amber on today, theme accent on upcoming, neutral on completed + untimed. The eye picks up urgency without having to read each date column.

Screenshot-driven cleanup, Patch A — eight quick wins that ship together because each touches one file. Inspector "Defer until: Available now" rephrased to "Not deferred"; the Builder subsection rename to "Schedule depth"; "Inbox" project chip suppressed when already viewing Inbox; window title reflects the active view ("Atrium · Today" etc.); fixture areas + tags cycle through the six-swatch palette so the per-area stripe and Pango tag pills demonstrate themselves without manual setup; emoji prefixes dropped from fixture-generated titles (those were title text masquerading as derived state); main-content AdwClamp bumped from 720 → 960 px to reclaim the dead zone on wide windows with the inspector pane visible.

Soft-accent pass — adds a thin layer of accent warmth across six surfaces (sidebar background, header bars, page title, sidebar count badges, section headers, inspector group headings, and task-row hover) so the UI reads as warmer and more curated without any single surface screaming. Uses libadwaita's named colour tokens, so light / dark / high-contrast themes stay in lockstep. CSS-only patch — no code changes.

Two startup-log warning fixes surfaced by an eyes-on test of v0.6.8. A no-op placeholder CSS rule (v0.6.1) used a pseudo-element selector GTK4 doesn't recognise — replaced with a comment, since the rule never rendered anything. And the main search bar's SearchEntry wasn't explicitly connected to the SearchBar (it lives inside a wrapper Box for the `?` help button, so auto-discovery missed it), causing a "search bar does not have an entry connected" warning on every key event. One-line `bar.connect_entry(&entry)` fix.

v0.6.x cleanup pass — end-of-session maintenance. Eleven patches shipped since the v0.5.0 line and the contract docs lagged behind. spec.md, roadmap.md, and README.md now reflect what actually shipped: Slice D end-to-end (kanban renderer + Agenda canonical page), FTS5 bm25 + recency ranking, SQL-translation evaluator, sidebar top-tier reorg. spec.md adds three new subsections (§4.4 Bm25, §4.5 SQL-translation, §4.6 Perspective renderers) without renumbering the existing tail. roadmap.md Phase 15.75 rewritten as a "what shipped where" map. Plus small code hygiene — dead arguments removed, stale Phase-X-will-do comments cleaned. Workspace clippy clean; ship-gate green.

Sidebar reorganisation. Agenda / Forecast / Review no longer hide at the bottom of the Builder section — they now sit in the top tier alongside Inbox / Today / etc., each with its own subtle icon tint (red for the urgent alarm of Agenda, blue for the calendar of Forecast, green for the checkmark of Review). Agenda is mode-agnostic and appears in both Simple and Builder; Forecast and Review stay Builder-only but join the top tier when visible. The "Perspectives" section moves up to right under the top-tier group — above Areas, below the Inbox grouping. The "Builder" sidebar header is gone.

Kanban drag-drop CPU mitigation. Two targeted optimisations: dropped the transition: background-color 120ms on board / agenda task rows (each cursor-crossed row was firing a CSS animation during drag motion, producing continuous repaint work), and wired the v0.5.3 SQL translation evaluator into the GUI's board refresh path so the per-drop rebuild loads only matching rows instead of pulling every task and filtering in Rust. At 10K+ tasks the SQL fast-path dominates; at fixture scale it's a measurable but smaller win. The hover background still applies — it's just instant now.

atrium-cli perspective write side — closes the gap that the only way to create or convert a saved perspective from the shell was via direct SQL. Three new sub-subcommands: perspective create NAME --filter EXPR …, perspective edit NAME … (with --rename / --filter / --icon / --renderer / --columns), and perspective delete NAME. Name lookup is case-insensitive exact on write paths so a typo can't accidentally edit the wrong row. --renderer board requires --columns on create; on edit, --columns alone updates an existing board's column list in place. The regression ship-gate now exercises the full create → board conversion → list conversion → delete round-trip.

Slice D2 — the Agenda canonical page. Org-mode-style chronological view that groups open tasks into Overdue / Today / Tomorrow / This Week / Next Week sections. Tasks farther out live in Forecast; tasks without a time anchor (no scheduled, no deadline) don't appear. The Overdue heading is rendered in red so urgency reads at a glance. Each row shows title + date chip + project + tag pills, and clicking opens the Inspector. Available alongside Forecast and Review in the Builder-mode sidebar.

Kanban drag-drop between columns. The kanban is no longer read-only — drag a task row from one column to another and its tag set is rewritten so the grouper buckets it under the new column. The rewrite removes the source column tag, adds the destination column tag (if not already present), and preserves any non-column tags. Dropping on the trailing "Other" column just removes the source column tag. Pure-Rust tag-rewrite logic in atrium-core (eight unit tests) so the GUI stays a thin GTK4 DnD adapter on top.

Perspective renderer dialog — closes the v0.6.0 gap that the only way to make a Perspective render as a kanban was direct SQL or the test fixture. Right- clicking a Perspective row in the sidebar now opens a "Configure renderer…" dialog: pick List or Board, edit the column list (comma-separated tag names), save. The active perspective re-renders immediately when the worker emits the library-changes delta.

Kanban polish — board rows now show a metadata line with the project name, the most-relevant date (deadline if set, else scheduled, else Someday), and tag pills using the same Pango-coloured markup the regular task list uses. The completion checkbox is interactive: clicking it toggles the task via the worker, same as the rest of the app. Tasks with no metadata stay tight — the secondary line is suppressed entirely rather than rendering empty.

Slice D1 GUI — saved Perspectives whose renderer is "board" now render as a horizontal kanban layout in the main window instead of a flat list. Each column is a tag (leftmost match wins, trailing "Other" bucket for tasks that don't match any configured column), and clicking a task row opens it in the Inspector. Read-only at v0.6.0: drag-drop between columns, in-board completion toggles, and a board-renderer editing dialog all follow in a sibling patch — for now, moving tasks between columns is atrium-cli edit ID --tag X --remove-tag Y or the Inspector's tag editor. Same grouping engine the v0.5.4 atrium-cli kanban subcommand uses, so the GUI and CLI stay in lockstep.

Slice D1 foundation — saved Perspectives can render as kanban boards. v0.5.4 ships the headless engine: a renderer parser, a tag-axis grouping helper, and a complete atrium-cli kanban NAME subcommand that exercises the renderer end-to-end from the shell. The GUI consumer lands in v0.6.0. Three locked rules keep the kanban tight: leftmost-match-wins per task, an unconditional "Other" trailing column for tasks that don't match any configured column, and case-insensitive tag matching to mirror the rest of the search engine.

SQL-translation evaluator for the Calibre-style search expression language — the fourth v0.6.x carryover off the deferred list. Queries that map cleanly onto SQL (boolean composition, bare text, state predicates, title/note/tag matches, date comparisons / ranges, numeric estimated comparisons) now execute at the SQLite layer instead of pulling every row into memory. Anything not yet expressible in SQL — regex (tag:~), fuzzy (tag:?), sequential-project state, the composite is:today / is:upcoming family — falls back to the in-memory evaluator. The two paths are pinned to identical behaviour by 21 parity integration tests. Atrium-cli wires the fast-path for v1; the GUI search-bar follows in a sibling patch.

FTS5 bm25 + recency ranking on bare-text searches — the third v0.6.x carryover off the deferred list. Searching for a freeform word (in the search bar or via atrium-cli search milk) now ranks the matches by FTS5's bm25 blended with a 30-day half-life recency factor. Stronger matches and freshly-touched tasks rise to the top instead of every result coming back in task-position order. Skipped automatically when the query carries an explicit sort: modifier — power users keep their pinned ordering.

Patch release. Three small, coupled fixes the v0.5.0 ship-gate hadn't been wide enough to catch: the atrium-cli runtime nesting panic on every write subcommand (Cannot start a runtime from within a runtime), a broken-pipe panic when piping output into head or other early-closing consumers, and a regression-script step that exercises atrium-cli end-to-end against a fixture database so the next ship-gate catches the same shape of bug.

A meaty minor — fifteen post-v0.4.0 patches rolled together into one shippable boundary. Phase 15.5 deferred-list closed: state predicates, sort: modifier, fuzzy ? match, search history, and an operator-reference popover. Architectural extraction: the search engine now lives as its own workspace crate atrium-search, and a complete headless CLI atrium-cli ships alongside the GTK binary — every non-GUI surface is now exercisable from the shell. Phase 15.75 visual polish (partial): visual rhythm pass, per-area accent stripes, canonical-list icon tinting, About-dialog icon resolution. Closes the v0.4.0 deferred bug list.

Phase 15.5 — Calibre-powered search. The search bar's filter language grew from a flat tag:foo is:open shape into a full Calibre-style expression grammar: boolean operators (AND / OR / NOT), parens grouping, comparison operators on date and numeric fields (due:>today, estimated:>=30), inclusive ranges (due:2026-05-01..2026-05-31), date keywords (thisweek, lastmonth, 5daysago), state predicates (is:overdue, is:repeating, is:tagged), and Calibre-style match modifiers (tag:x substring, tag:=x exact, tag:~x regex, tag:true/false existence). Saved Perspectives inherit the new grammar for free since they store filter expressions verbatim. The full operator reference lives in spec §4.3.

Visual polish pass. Tag colours land — six-swatch picker in the tag editor, coloured dots in the sidebar, coloured pills on every task row. Task rows light up gently on hover; completing a task gives the checkbox a brief celebratory pop. Per-list empty states get warmer copy and the right icon (Someday gets a moon, no more weird house glyph). Project headings now show the area breadcrumb so you know where you are in the hierarchy. The Builder Mode Inspector pane reads as its own sheet with a subtle left border.

Audit-pass bug fixes. Filter typos in the search bar (or in a saved Perspective) now surface a toast instead of silently no-op-ing — type tga:work by mistake and you'll see "Unknown filter" rather than empty results. New empty- library hint at the bottom of the sidebar nudges first-run users toward "New Project" when there's nothing in their library yet. Inbox tasks now read "Inbox" in the Area › Project context chip on Today and similar views, rather than rendering blank. Screen-reader experience improved: count badges now announce as "5 open tasks" instead of bare "5".

Two task-row fixes. Adding or removing a tag in the per-task editor now updates the row's tag pill in place instead of leaving stale text. And task rows on Today, Inbox, Anytime, and other cross-list views now show an "Area › Project" chip so you can place each task in your hierarchy at a glance.

Builder Mode milestone — Phase 15 lands repeating tasks with full RFC 5545 RRULE support. Set a repeat in the Inspector (Daily / Weekly / Monthly / Yearly preset, or a custom RRULE string), pick the Org-mode behavior — Cumulative (the default — spawn the next instance after now), From-completion-date (anchor on when you finished), or Always-shift (basic). When you complete a repeating task, Atrium spawns the next instance in place with all dates shifted, tags carried forward, and project / parent preserved. Backwards-compatible 0003_repeat_mode.sql migration adds the mode column to task.

v0.2.0 also runs the sanctioned major-bump maintenance pass: dead Builder-stub helper removed, four duplicated dummy-task test helpers consolidated behind a new atrium-core test-support feature gate. Spec §4.4 schema-freeze rule formally amended to "no breaking mid-v0.1 changes" — purely-additive new tables and columns are now allowed.

Phase 14 — saved Perspectives. Save the current search bar query as a named view from the primary menu, and it lands in a dedicated Perspectives section in the Builder sidebar. Each perspective re-runs its filter expression every time it's opened, so it stays current with the underlying tasks. Right-click a perspective to rename or delete it. Adds a backwards-compatible 0002_perspectives.sql migration; existing databases upgrade in place.

Phase 13 — Review queue. Builder Mode's GTD discipline: projects with a configured review interval surface in the Review sidebar entry when their last review is older than the interval allows. Each card shows the project title, area, how stale the review is, and a Mark Reviewed button that stamps the timestamp and drops the row out of the queue. The Phase 10 Review stub is gone.

Fix: fast double-clicks now reliably trigger inline title edit. GtkListView's internal click gesture was claiming the event sequence on fast doubles to fire its own activate signal, which blocked our row-level gesture from seeing the second release. Now listening to GtkListView::activate directly so the path GTK uses internally for double-click activation is the path we use too. The per-row gesture stays in place to catch slower doubles that fall outside GTK's window.

Fix: double-click → inline title edit was firing the edit but the editor was closing before users saw it. Cause: the click event was still propagating when our gesture's handler ran; GtkListView's internal selection handler grabbed focus on the row *after* we'd grabbed focus on the entry, triggering the entry's focus-leave handler which committed and closed the editor. Deferred the edit-start to an idle callback so it runs after the event settles, and our grab_focus is the last word.

Double-click hardening: 700 ms time window widened to 800 ms, plus the gesture now bails out when the row is already in edit mode. Stops fast double-clicks from bouncing the cursor and resetting the entry text after the editor opens.

Fix: double-clicking a task row now reliably enters inline title edit. The previous detection used GTK's built-in n_press counter which only matches clicks inside the system's gtk-double-click-time setting (~400 ms by default) — too tight for natural trackpad double-clicks. Replaced with our own 700 ms time-window detection so the gesture matches user intent rather than the system threshold.

Diagnostic build for the double-click-not-firing report. The per-row activate gesture moved from Bubble to Capture propagation phase so it fires before the parent GtkListItemWidget's selection-handling can consume the event. Plus debug-level tracing on both the gesture handler and start_edit_on_row so the trace output tells us exactly which step is failing.

Double-click a task row now enters inline title edit (same surface as F2). Single click still selects and holds focus. The Inspector dialog stays accessible via Ctrl+I and right-click → Edit Details… — double-click is the lighter "just rename this" path. Things-3 idiom.

Fix: double-clicking a task in Simple Mode now reliably opens the modal Inspector dialog. open_inspector_for was still reading the GSettings mode string directly, which v0.1.6 caught as unreliable across Settings wrapper instances. Migrated every same-frame mode read to the synchronous Cell tracker that apply_mode writes to first; the only GSettings reads that remain are the startup initial-mode and the safety-net observer's callback, both of which flow into apply_mode and populate the Cell.

The bulk-action toolbar above the task list now reveals only when 2 or more rows are selected. Single-row selection had the row checkbox + Delete key + Ctrl+I covering everything, so the toolbar's Complete and Delete buttons were just redundant duplicates of those. The toolbar still appears for true multi-select bulk ops.

Fix: switching to Builder Mode via the menu now reliably shows the side pane on the same frame as the click. The window's GSettings change observer wasn't firing on the process's own writes — likely two separate Settings wrappers and the dconf backend not crossing the changed signal between them. The menu action now calls window.apply_mode directly after persisting the GSetting, and the observer stays as a safety net for external writes (dconf-editor and similar).

Fix: the Inspector side pane now reliably populates when you select a task in Builder Mode. The v0.1.5 mode-gate in the selection-changed handler was reading the GSettings string directly, which sometimes returned a stale value on the same frame as a mode flip. Replaced with a synchronous Cell tracker that apply_mode writes before any other work happens. Plus debug-level tracing on every branch of the pane-refresh path so the next bug report comes with data.

Fix: the Inspector side pane now reliably hides when switching from Builder Mode back to Simple. The OverlaySplitView's show-sidebar property alone wasn't sufficient — apply_mode now also hides the pane host explicitly and clears its content, and the selection- changed handler bails out in Simple Mode so it can't repopulate the editor for an invisible pane.

Fix: Ctrl+I, double-click, and right-click → Edit Details now consistently open the editor in Builder Mode by routing the targeted task into the side pane and grabbing keyboard focus on its title row. The v0.1.1 design call that made these no-ops in Builder was wrong — the user's mental model of Ctrl+I is "get me into the editor for this task," not "open another editor on top of one I'm already looking at." Simple Mode keeps opening the modal dialog as before.

Phase 12 — Forecast view. Builder Mode's calendar-axis layout: a scrollable column of day cards over the next 30 days, each showing scheduled / deadlined / defer-ending tasks with reason chips. Today's card gets an accent border; an Overdue pseudo-block at the top surfaces past-due open tasks. Drag a task between day cards to reschedule it. The Phase 10 Forecast stub is gone.

Phase 11 — defer dates and sequential project rendering. The Inspector (modal + side pane) gains a working "Defer until" date picker; tasks deferred to a future date drop out of Today and Anytime until the date crosses. In a sequential project, only the first incomplete task renders at full opacity; the rest dim with a `.queued` CSS class. Sidebar project badges in Builder Mode show the *available* count (1 for sequential, N for parallel) instead of the raw open count. The Phase 10 placeholder for `estimated_minutes` becomes a live SpinRow that dispatches to the worker.

Phase 10 — Builder Mode UI shell. The mode switch becomes real: the primary menu Mode → Builder toggle now reveals an Inspector side pane on the right (auto-saving as you edit), Builder-only sidebar entries (Forecast, Review, Perspectives) appear as stub placeholders pointing at the phases that fill them in, and project pages gain a Sequential toggle and Review interval picker. Per spec §3.1 mode-as-view, no DB work happens on mode switch — enforced by the new mode-flip snapshot integration test.
First public release. Simple Mode is feature-complete:

ul.li: Inbox · Today · Upcoming · Anytime · Someday · Logbook
Areas → Projects → Tasks hierarchy with multi-tag
Scheduled (When) and Deadline distinction; Today picks up both overdue items and the next 7 days of deadlines
Quick Entry global capture (Ctrl+Alt+Space) with inline #tag / @today / @deadline syntax
FTS5-backed search with filter expressions (tag:NAME · is:open|done|overdue · due:today)
Inspector and tag editor for the full per-task fieldset
Multi-select with bulk Complete / Delete and Ctrl+Z undo
Find-as-you-type sidebar filter (Ctrl+L)
Keyboard-first — every common op bound, mouse optional
Local-first storage at $XDG_DATA_HOME/atrium/atrium.db (single-writer SQLite worker, WAL mode); no network, no telemetry, no accounts
High-legibility accessibility toggle (Atkinson Hyperlegible)

description.p: The schema is the OmniFocus superset on day one — Builder Mode (v0.2) will expose the columns Simple Mode hides; no migration on mode switch.

Today filter widens to include upcoming-deadline heads-up. Spec §4.2 picks up a `today + N` deadline window (default 7 days), so a task with a deadline approaching surfaces in Today before it's actually due — the behaviour Things 3 users expect. Earlier versions left future-deadlined tasks buried in Anytime until the deadline date arrived.

UI bugsweep before v0.1.0. Inspector + tag editor switch from free-standing windows to in-window AdwDialogs (the v0.0.36 form rendered as a transparent overlay because the parent window bled through). Task rows replace GtkEditableLabel with a Label/Entry stack so single-clicks on the title no longer hijack the row's selection / Inspector-open path. Esc moves from a window-global accel onto the task list's shortcut controller — typing in any entry no longer silently clears the multi-selection. Date pills stop ellipsising (no more "May" rows). Documentation refresh in CLAUDE.md and keymap.md.
Phase 8a — typography polish + completion fade. Inter cv11/ss01 on UI labels, tabular figures audit, surface-by-surface size/weight pass, opacity fade for completed task rows.
Phase 7f — full keyboard map. Ctrl+Z invokes the active undo toast; F2 starts inline editing on the focused task row.
Phase 7e — find-as-you-type sidebar. GtkSearchEntry above the row list filters areas, projects, and tags; Ctrl+L focuses it.
Phase 7d — search filter expressions. tag:, is:, due: clauses mix with freeform text in the search bar.
Phase 7c — multi-select with Ctrl+Click / Shift+Click / Ctrl+A, bulk Complete and Delete actions.
Phase 7b — undo for toggle-complete and delete-task via 6 s AdwToast.
Phase 7a — FTS5-backed search bar (Ctrl+F).

Pre-implementation — the contract, public framing, and metadata land before any code. Phases 0 through 6 wire up the workspace, data layer, UI shell, and Simple Mode core (Inbox/Today, Areas, Projects, Tags, Quick Entry).
